title: Developer vows to present more ‘options’ for Davis Square tower project amid neighborhood opposition
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Developer Copper Mill plans to present new design options for the Davis Square tower project in response to neighborhood opposition.
The proposed 26-story building includes 502 apartments, with 126 designated as affordable housing units.
Residents at the meeting expressed concerns about the project's size and compatibility with Davis Square's character.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for his 2 nephews with down syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Steven Ponte, 37, from Somerville, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for his nephews with Down syndrome.
Ponte aims to honor his late nephew Sebastian and support his nephew Zachary's future.
The Massachusetts Down Syndrome Congress supported Ponte's family through hospitalizations and continues to inspire his marathon run.

title: Boston firefighter, National Guard member reportedly injured in strike that killed 6 U.S. soldiers
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Major Mark Garcia, a Boston firefighter and National Guard member, was injured in an Iranian drone strike in Kuwait.
Six U.S. soldiers were killed in the drone strike where Garcia was injured; he is recovering in Germany.
Garcia is affiliated with Engine 50 in Charlestown and praised by Local 718 for his dedication to service.

title: Lowell murder trial delayed after lead State Police investigator allegedly causes fatal crash while driving drunk
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The retrial for the Phan brothers, accused in a 2020 Lowell murder, is delayed indefinitely.
State Police Sgt. Scott Quigley's alleged drunk driving crash led to complications in the trial schedule.
Quigley's investigation status and legal proceedings are crucial to the trial's timeline and outcome.

title: ​Bruins’ playoff hopes might rest on Jeremy Swayman’s shoulders for this stretch run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jeremy Swayman has a .906 save percentage through 42 games, an improvement from last season's .892.
Swayman's strong goaltending is key as the Bruins fight for a playoff spot despite defensive struggles.
The Bruins have a 13-game home win streak, with Swayman 9-0 at TD Garden in 2026, boasting a .939 save rate.

title: Watch: Body cam shows Patriots party at downtown Boston restaurant
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Boston police body cam footage shows a party with Patriots players at Estella restaurant featuring hookahs and strippers.
Police found unauthorized adult entertainment and after-hours drinking, leading to a licensing board suspension for Estella's owner.
No arrests were made, but the restaurant owner faces a three-day license suspension with potential further action.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots safety Kevin Byard
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Patriots signed safety Kevin Byard to a one-year, $9 million contract after an All-Pro season with the Bears.
Byard, 33, led the NFL with seven interceptions in 2025 and has 36 career interceptions over 10 seasons.
Known for his durability, Byard has not missed a game in his NFL career, playing 99.8% of snaps in 2025.

title: Man resentenced in 2011 slaying of Dorchester teen
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Nyasani Watt resentenced to 14-17 years for the 2011 fatal shooting of a Dorchester teen, eligible for parole.
Watt's original murder conviction overturned by the Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court due to denial of right to counsel.
Another involved, Sheldon Mattis, initially sentenced to life without parole, now serving with potential for parole after a court decision.

title: Samuel Adams launches new ‘Our City. Our Beer.’ variety pack inspired by Boston sports stars
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Samuel Adams releases 'Our City. Our Beer.' variety pack inspired by Boston athletes Derrick White, Garrett Crochet, Julian Edelman, and Zdeno Chara.
The variety pack includes four themed beers: White Ale, Crochet’s Pale Ale, Julian Edel-Brau Lager, and Big Z Pilsner.
A launch party at Banners Kitchen & Tap in Boston on April 4 will feature the athletes serving their respective beers.

title: Boston Marathon race director to run with his 3 kids — and astronaut Sunita Williams
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Dave McGillivray will run his 54th Boston Marathon with his three children and retired astronaut Sunita Williams.
McGillivray will coordinate the race start in Hopkinton before joining the marathon with his kids.
McGillivray's family members are dedicating their races to various causes, including medical care and prosthetic running blades.
